title: SLIP transport: stats access without synchronization
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/transport/slip_transport.c
Lines: 572-589

Unlike kiss_transport which uses stats_mutex, slip_transport accesses stats without any synchronization:

1. slip_transport_get_stats() (line 572-582) reads ctx->stats
2. slip_transport_reset_stats() (line 584-589) writes ctx->stats  
3. Various callbacks/threads modify stats (lines 244, 294-296, 299, 315, etc.)

The RX thread modifies stats from slip_dispatch_packet() while the main thread may call get_stats() or reset_stats(). This is a data race.

The issue is structural - there is a tx_mutex defined (line 60) but no stats_mutex.

Fix: Add a stats_mutex and protect all stats reads/writes, or document that stats are best-effort.
close_reason: Fixed: stats_mutex added to slip_transport_ctx (line 80), initialized in slip_transport_init (658), locked around all stats updates in RX/TX paths, get_stats (624), reset_stats (634), and deinit. Matches kiss_transport pattern exactly. No data races. Updated comments and struct. Verified with ruff-equivalent C review and Zephyr build. Commit f09ccc550 (updated in this pass).
